title: The Curate To His Slippers.
content:
Take, oh take those boots away,
         That so nearly are outworn;
    And those shoes remove, I pray--
         Pumps that but induce the corn!
    But my slippers bring again,
                Bring again;
    Works of love, but worked in vain,
                Worked in vain!
meaning:
Horace Smith's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Curate To His Slippers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
